title: --log-opt fails to parse tag
body:
### Issue Description

This works and shows the label properly:
```
$ podman inspect ubuntu --format '{{index  .Config.Labels "org.opencontainers.image.ref.name" }}'
ubuntu
```
Running:
```
$ podman run --rm -it --log-opt 'tag={{index  .Config.Labels "org.opencontainers.image.ref.name" }}' ubuntu echo 33
```
fails with:
```
Error: invalid argument "tag={{index  .Config.Labels \"org.opencontainers.image.ref.name\" }}" for "--log-opt" flag: parse error on line 1, column 29: bare " in non-quoted-field
See 'podman run --help'
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Execute `podman run --rm -it --log-opt 'tag={{index  .Config.Labels "org.opencontainers.image.ref.name" }}' ubuntu echo 33`

### Describe the results you received

Error:
```
Error: invalid argument "tag={{index  .Config.Labels \"org.opencontainers.image.ref.name\" }}" for "--log-opt" flag: parse error on line 1, column 29: bare " in non-quoted-field
See 'podman run --help'
```


### Describe the results you expected

I'd expect it to show `ubuntu`

### Additional information

Sounds awfully similar to:
 * https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/2574
 * https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/2717
 * https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/2582

comments.body:
Hmm, I believe the syntax is `podman run --rm -it --log-opt tag="{{.Config.Labels.org.opencontainers.image.ref.name}}" ubuntu echo 33`, where the quotes go after the =, and not enclosing tag. 

Let me know if this works.

It "works" in the sense that it throws no error but it also returns no value -- which is somewhat expected since `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` is the key and not something nested… (This is why I had index above).

But moving the quotes around to be like `podman run --rm -it --log-opt tag='{{index  .Config.Labels "org.opencontainers.image.ref.name" }}' ubuntu echo 33` doesn't make it work either.

This doesn't seem to be a problem with podman, the error is thrown by the cli parser library.
It looks like it uses the golang internal csv parser since we defined this flag as StringSlice instead of StringArray.
https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/spf13/pflag@v1.0.5#FlagSet.StringSlice vs https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/spf13/pflag@v1.0.5#FlagSet.StringArray

Now because it uses csv to parse that it uses csv rules. A field using quotes must be a) in quotes and b) quotes inside must be escaped with another quote. So the correct string should look like this:
```
--log-opt '"tag={{index  .Config.Labels ""org.opencontainers.image.ref.name"" }}"'
```

Now that just looks stupid IMO and we should use StringArray instead but that seems like a much larger change effecting many more options. And of course if you now use a value like that and we change it to StringArray that would be a backwards incompatible change.
Indeed, that does work. I'll leave it up to you to close the issue if you think it cannot be done due to backwards compat.

I think it something that needs to be investigated carefully. There are certainly options were this behaviour is preferred, for example --cap-add and --cap-drop, there using a comma separated list makes a lot of sense.

We can do breaking changes with 5.0 so we might need to wait a bit but I rather fix this if we get the chance there.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
